title: Multi-task next-lap plus stop-horizon model
summary: Recent F1 pit-stop work reports a cascaded multi-task setup where next-lap stop classification is helped by estimating laps until the next stop.
rationale: Recent F1 pit-stop work reports a cascaded multi-task setup where next-lap stop classification is helped by estimating laps until the next stop. That auxiliary target should regularize the boundary between 'stop now' and 'stop soon', which the current pure binary stack does not model explicitly.
implementation_hint: Create an auxiliary label such as `LapsToNextPit` within each driver-race sequence, fit a second model or joint sequence net, and feed OOF horizon predictions plus horizon uncertainty buckets back into the main XGB/Cat blend. Test both direct multi-output NN and two-stage tabular stacking.
expected_effect: Better separation of imminent stops from medium-horizon stops; likely small but real AUC gain if current errors cluster around 1-3 laps before the stop.
risk: Auxiliary labels are noisy near censored race endings and safety-car distortions; careless construction can leak future information.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
